questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

The PLI scheme is aimed at:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: project / the / completed / was / time / on
options: The project was completed on time
Was the project completed on time
Completed was the project on time
The project was completed on time
On time the project was completed
questions.solution: The project was completed on time

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

According to the passage, India has the ____ largest coal reserves in the world.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: plays / role / coal / in / energy / a / vital / production / India
options: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production
Vital role coal plays in energy production India
In India coal plays a energy vital production role
Coal in India plays vital role energy production
Coal plays a vital role in India energy production
questions.solution: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Continuous miners and longwall mining methods have increased:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: innovation / for / key / is / growth / economic
options: Innovation is key for economic growth
Innovation is key for economic growth
Economic growth is key for innovation
For economic growth innovation is key
Key for growth economic is innovation
questions.solution: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a risk in underground mining?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: climate / urgent / change / requires / action / addressing
options: Addressing climate change requires urgent action
Climate change addressing urgent action requires
Addressing climate change requires urgent action
Requires urgent action change addressing climate
Urgent action climate change requires addressing
questions.solution: Addressing climate change requires urgent action

India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The word 'abundant' in the passage most nearly means:

questions.stem: Rearrange: data / protect / essential / is / privacy / to
options: Essential is to protect data privacy
Data privacy is essential to protect

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

What percentage of India's coal output does CIL account for?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: education / the / to / key / is / success
options: Education is the key to success
To success the key is education
Success is the key to education
Education is the key to success
questions.solution: Education is the key to success

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's solar power capacity in 2024 is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: company / its / announced / expansion / has / plans / the
options: The company has announced its expansion plans
The company has announced its expansion plans
Has the company announced its expansion plans
Expansion plans the company has announced
Announced the company its expansion plans has
questions.solution: The company has announced its expansion plans

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERILOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
options: Neither he nor his brother were going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother have been going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.
questions.solution: Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The price of (A) / these goods (B) / have increased (C) / significantly. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
options: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.
He is one who has excelled in the field.
He is one of those who excelled in the field.
He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
questions.solution: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The company (A) / is in (B) / the red (C) / for three years. (D)
options: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'
questions.solution: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is the tallest of the two sisters.
options: She is the taller of the two sisters.
She is the tallest of the two sisters.
She is more tall of the two sisters.
She is tall among the two sisters.
questions.solution: She is the taller of the two sisters.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The car (A) / which he (B) / is driving (C) / is a Mercedes. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FERVENT:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A photodiode is normally operated in:
questions.solution: Reverse-biased photodiodes produce a current proportional to incident light.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier series can represent any periodic signal as a sum of:
questions.solution: A periodic signal decomposes into a fundamental plus its harmonics.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC low-pass filter has R = 1 kΩ and C = 10 nF. Its cut-off frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The total voltage gain of two amplifier stages with gains A₁ and A₂ in cascade is:
questions.solution: Cascaded voltage gains multiply (or add in dB): A_total = A₁·A₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 0, B = 0, C = 1. The output Y is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="30" x2="60" y2="30" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="55" x2="60" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="34" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">A=0</text><text x="4" y="59" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">B=0</text><path d="M60 18 L85 18 Q105 42 85 66 L60 66 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="74" y="46" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">AND</text><line x1="105" y1="42" x2="140" y2="42" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="140" y2="95" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="99" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">C=1</text><path d="M140 30 Q150 30 165 55 Q150 80 140 80 Q150 55 140 30 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">OR</text><line x1="165" y1="55" x2="220" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="195" y="48" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Y=?</text></svg>
figure.caption: AND–OR logic circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine system stability without:
questions.solution: Routh's array tests sign changes in the first column to count right-half-plane roots directly.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bipolar junction transistor (BJT) is fundamentally a:
questions.solution: Collector current is controlled by base current (Ic = βIb) — a current-controlled device.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An amplifier has a voltage gain of 1000. Expressed in decibels this is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a BJT to operate as a linear amplifier, its junctions must be biased so that the:
options: Emitter junction is reverse and collector forward biased
Emitter junction is forward and collector junction reverse biased
questions.solution: Active (amplifying) region: emitter–base forward, collector–base reverse biased.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 10 kΩ and Rin = 5 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a sinusoidally modulated AM wave, the maximum fraction of total power in the sidebands (at 100% modulation) is:
questions.solution: At μ = 1, sideband power is Pc·μ²/2 of carrier; total sideband fraction = μ²/(2+μ²) = 1/3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ideal operational amplifier has:
options: Infinite open-loop gain and input impedance
questions.solution: Ideal op-amp: infinite gain, infinite Zin, zero Zout, infinite bandwidth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many bits are needed to encode 8 distinct quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ALU in a CPU performs:
questions.solution: The Arithmetic Logic Unit executes add/subtract and AND/OR/NOT-type operations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 80 V is modulated by a 50 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A high common-mode rejection ratio (CMRR) in a differential amplifier means it:
options: Strongly rejects signals common to both inputs
questions.solution: CMRR = differential gain/common-mode gain; high CMRR suppresses noise common to both inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region of convergence (ROC) of the Laplace transform determines:
options: Whether the system is stable and/or causal
questions.solution: Stability requires the ROC to include the jω-axis; ROC position also encodes causality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 0, B = 1, C = 1. The output Y is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="30" x2="60" y2="30" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="55" x2="60" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="34" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">A=0</text><text x="4" y="59" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">B=1</text><path d="M60 18 L85 18 Q105 42 85 66 L60 66 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="74" y="46" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">AND</text><line x1="105" y1="42" x2="140" y2="42" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="140" y2="95" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="99" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">C=1</text><path d="M140 30 Q150 30 165 55 Q150 80 140 80 Q150 55 140 30 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">OR</text><line x1="165" y1="55" x2="220" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="195" y="48" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Y=?</text></svg>
figure.caption: AND–OR logic circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse-code modulation (PCM) involves sampling, quantizing and:
questions.solution: PCM digitizes an analog signal by sampling, quantizing, then binary-encoding each sample.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The rectified output waveform shown (every half-cycle is positive) is produced by a:
questions.solution: Both half-cycles appear as positive humps with no gaps — characteristic of full-wave rectification.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 110" width="250" height="110"><line x1="15" y1="90" x2="235" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 90 Q 45 20 70 90 Q 95 20 120 90 Q 145 20 170 90 Q 195 20 220 90" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="222" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: full-wave rectified output

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sampling a signal below the Nyquist rate causes:
options: Aliasing (high frequencies fold to lower ones)
questions.solution: Under-sampling overlaps spectral images, so high frequencies masquerade as lower ones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A multiplexer (MUX) is a circuit that:
options: Splits one input into many outputs
Selects one of several inputs onto a single output
questions.solution: A MUX routes one of 2ⁿ inputs to the output based on n select lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 100 kΩ and Rin = 2 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A p-type semiconductor is produced by doping silicon with a:
questions.solution: Trivalent atoms (e.g. boron) create holes, giving p-type material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 150 has a base current of 20 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A decoder with n input lines activates:
questions.solution: An n-to-2ⁿ decoder asserts exactly one output line for each input combination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In frequency modulation (FM), the carrier parameter varied is its:
questions.solution: FM varies the instantaneous carrier frequency with the message amplitude.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 4-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM is generally preferred over AM for high-fidelity audio because it offers:
questions.solution: FM's constant amplitude makes it far more robust against amplitude noise than AM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 1 µH and C = 100 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In amplitude modulation (AM), the quantity varied in proportion to the message is the carrier's:
questions.solution: AM varies the carrier amplitude with the modulating signal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cut-off frequency of a simple RC low-pass filter is:
questions.solution: f_c = 1/(2πRC); above it the output falls at 20 dB/decade.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 159 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 159 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 120" width="270" height="120"><line x1="25" y1="90" x2="255" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="95" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M30 30 L150 30 L240 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="90" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="104" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f_c</text><text x="34" y="26" font-size="9" fill="#475569">|H|(dB)</text><text x="244" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">log f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Low-pass magnitude response

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The wavelength λ of an electromagnetic wave of frequency f in free space is:
questions.solution: λ = c/f, with c the speed of light.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 100 has a base current of 40 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PN-junction diode conducts appreciably only when it is:
questions.solution: Forward bias lowers the barrier and allows current; reverse bias blocks it (except small leakage).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectifier circuit is used to convert:
questions.solution: Rectifiers (using diodes) convert alternating voltage into unidirectional (DC) voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two amplifiers of 15 dB and 30 dB are connected with a 3 dB interstage loss. The overall gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A half-adder produces which two outputs?
questions.solution: A half-adder gives Sum = A⊕B and Carry = A·B.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 5 kΩ, Rf = 10 kΩ, Vin = 2 V), the output voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LED emits light when it is:
questions.solution: Forward-biased recombination of carriers releases photons in an LED.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in a control system primarily improves:
questions.solution: Negative feedback reduces sensitivity to disturbances and improves accuracy and stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM signal carries a message of highest frequency 4 kHz. Its transmission bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Zener diode used as a voltage regulator operates in its:
questions.solution: In reverse breakdown the Zener holds a nearly constant voltage, regulating the output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 1101₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Shannon–Hartley theorem gives channel capacity as:
questions.solution: Capacity rises with bandwidth B and signal-to-noise ratio: C = B log₂(1 + SNR) bits/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A continuous LTI system is stable if all poles of its transfer function lie in the:
questions.solution: Poles with negative real parts (left half-plane) give decaying, stable responses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A summing op-amp (Rf = 10 kΩ) has inputs 2 V via 2 kΩ and 1 V via 10 kΩ. The output is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carson's rule estimates FM bandwidth as:
questions.solution: B_FM ≈ 2(Δf + fm), where Δf is peak deviation and fm the highest message frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 5 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A field-effect transistor (FET) is a:
options: Voltage-controlled device with high input impedance
questions.solution: Gate voltage controls the channel current; the gate draws almost no current (high Zin).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An antenna is a device that:
options: Converts between guided signals and radiated EM waves
questions.solution: Antennas transduce between currents on a conductor and free-space electromagnetic waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A non-inverting op-amp has Rf = 10 kΩ and Rin = 2 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Laplace transform is especially useful for analysing:
options: Transient and stability behaviour of systems
questions.solution: The s-domain handles initial conditions, transients and pole locations for stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 3-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an op-amp, the gain–bandwidth product is:
questions.solution: As closed-loop gain rises, bandwidth falls so that gain × bandwidth ≈ constant (unity-gain frequency).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slew rate of an op-amp limits the:
options: Maximum rate of change of the output voltage
questions.solution: Slew rate (V/µs) bounds how fast the output can swing, distorting fast large signals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 10 V and Amin = 4 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ADC is a circuit that converts:
options: An analog signal into a digital code
questions.solution: An analog-to-digital converter samples and quantizes an analog input into digital values.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 10 kΩ, Rf = 40 kΩ, Vin = 1 V), the output voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for wider bandwidth, lower distortion and better stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The output of a linear time-invariant (LTI) system is the input convolved with the:
questions.solution: y(t) = x(t) ∗ h(t); the impulse response h(t) fully characterizes an LTI system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 5 kΩ, Rf = 40 kΩ, Vin = 2 V), the output voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Over-modulation (μ > 1) in AM causes:
questions.solution: When μ > 1 the envelope crosses zero, distorting the recovered signal and spreading the spectrum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 50 V is modulated by a 20 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-channel enhancement MOSFET enters the saturation (active) region when:
options: V_GS > V_th and V_DS ≥ V_GS − V_th
questions.solution: Above threshold and with V_DS pinching off the channel, the MOSFET saturates and acts as a current source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The program counter (PC) in a microprocessor holds the:
questions.solution: The PC points to the memory address of the next instruction to fetch.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 0, B = 0, C = 0. The output Y is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an inverting op-amp circuit, the inverting input behaves as a:
questions.solution: Negative feedback forces the inverting input to ≈ 0 V (virtual ground) when the + input is grounded.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 1, B = 1, C = 1. The output Y is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="30" x2="60" y2="30" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="55" x2="60" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="34" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">A=1</text><text x="4" y="59" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">B=1</text><path d="M60 18 L85 18 Q105 42 85 66 L60 66 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="74" y="46" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">AND</text><line x1="105" y1="42" x2="140" y2="42" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="140" y2="95" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="99" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">C=1</text><path d="M140 30 Q150 30 165 55 Q150 80 140 80 Q150 55 140 30 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">OR</text><line x1="165" y1="55" x2="220" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="195" y="48" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Y=?</text></svg>
figure.caption: AND–OR logic circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-bit ADC/DAC provides how many distinct output levels?
questions.solution: n bits quantize into 2ⁿ levels; resolution = full-scale/2ⁿ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region around a PN junction that is depleted of free carriers is the:
questions.solution: Diffusion of carriers leaves a charge-depleted layer with a built-in potential.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 75 kHz and message bandwidth 15 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which gate gives an output of 1 only when both inputs differ?
questions.solution: XOR outputs 1 when the two inputs are unequal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A channel of bandwidth 10 kHz has an SNR of 20 dB. Its Shannon capacity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NAND and NOR are termed universal gates because they can:
questions.solution: Any logic function can be implemented using only NAND (or only NOR) gates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maxwell's equations predict that a time-varying electric field produces a:
questions.solution: Faraday/Ampère–Maxwell coupling of changing E and B fields gives rise to EM waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 900 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: According to the sampling theorem, a signal of maximum frequency fm must be sampled at a rate of at least:
questions.solution: The Nyquist rate is 2fm; sampling slower causes aliasing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM transmitter has modulation index μ = 0.8. The fraction of total power in the sidebands is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-type semiconductor is produced by doping silicon with a:
questions.solution: Pentavalent atoms (e.g. phosphorus) donate free electrons, giving n-type material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A flip-flop is the basic building block of:
questions.solution: Flip-flops store state and form registers, counters and other sequential logic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 10 µH and C = 100 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with a half-wave rectifier, a full-wave rectifier has:
questions.solution: Using both half-cycles, full-wave rectification gives smoother output and better efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 5 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The closed-loop gain of a non-inverting op-amp amplifier is:
questions.solution: Non-inverting gain = 1 + Rf/Rin, always ≥ 1 and in phase.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Frequency-division multiplexing (FDM) shares a channel by allocating:
options: Different frequency bands to different signals
questions.solution: FDM places each signal in its own frequency band on the shared medium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 15 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The closed-loop gain of an inverting op-amp amplifier is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 10 kΩ, Rf = 10 kΩ, Vin = 1 V), the output voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A MOSFET is preferred in digital ICs largely because of its:
options: Very high input impedance and low power
questions.solution: The insulated gate gives near-infinite input impedance and low static power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The bandwidth of an AM signal modulated by a message of highest frequency fm is:
questions.solution: AM produces two sidebands, giving a transmission bandwidth of 2fm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 10-bit PCM system has a signal-to-quantization-noise ratio of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time-division multiplexing (TDM) shares a channel by allocating:
options: Different time slots to different signals
questions.solution: TDM interleaves samples of several signals in distinct time slots on one channel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 600 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Class C amplifier achieves the highest efficiency but:
options: Conducts for less than half a cycle, producing heavy distortion
Conducts for the full cycle linearly
questions.solution: Class C conducts <180°, so it is efficient but non-linear — used with tuned RF loads.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The directivity (gain) of an antenna increases as its:
options: Effective aperture increases relative to wavelength
questions.solution: Gain ∝ 4π·A_eff/λ²; larger apertures (in wavelengths) give higher directivity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 12-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 10 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Barkhausen criterion for sustained oscillation requires the loop gain to be:
options: Unity with zero net phase shift
questions.solution: Oscillation is sustained when |loop gain| = 1 and total phase around the loop = 0°/360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 1000 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 1000 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 120" width="270" height="120"><line x1="25" y1="90" x2="255" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="95" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M30 30 L150 30 L240 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="90" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="104" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f_c</text><text x="34" y="26" font-size="9" fill="#475569">|H|(dB)</text><text x="244" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">log f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Low-pass magnitude response
